title: Security: Disable Administrator RDP login
description:
## Why

Leaving Administrator RDP access enabled is a security risk — direct admin login over RDP is a common attack vector.

## What

Disable RDP login for the Administrator account during provisioning.

## Open Question

Need to determine how to perform GUI operations that require Administrator after disabling RDP for that account. Hypothesis: a normal user with admin group membership can re-enable Administrator RDP access via elevated command prompt when needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Administrator account cannot log in via RDP after provisioning
- A documented procedure exists for temporarily re-enabling Administrator RDP access when required for GUI operations
- Provisioning step is idempotent
notes:
## References

- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/windows-client/windows-security/access-computer-after-administrator-disabled
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/manufacture/desktop/enable-and-disable-the-built-in-administrator-account?view=windows-11

## Key Finding

Enable/disable built-in Administrator account via elevated command prompt:

```cmd
net user administrator /active:yes
net user administrator /active:no
```

Normal user with admin privileges can run this to temporarily re-enable Administrator RDP access when needed for GUI operations.

title: Security: Install Windows updates during provisioning
description:
## Why

Freshly provisioned Windows instances may have unpatched vulnerabilities. Updates must be applied as early as provisioning allows to reduce attack surface.

## What

Add a provisioning step that installs all pending Windows updates on the host.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Windows updates installed automatically during provisioning
- Provisioning fails or warns if updates cannot be applied
- Step runs early enough in provisioning sequence to minimize exposure window

title: Reduce Docker image size with multi-stage build
description:
## Why

Current .devcontainer/Dockerfile uses a single stage — build tools (pip,
pipx, git, python3-venv, gcc etc.) end up in the runtime image, inflating
size unnecessarily. Large images slow CI pull times and increase storage cost.

## Solution

Adopt multi-stage build pattern from BadWinniePooh/ansible-collection
(.docker/Dockerfile, sha b51cf5f):

- **builder stage**: full toolchain — python3, pip, pipx, git, openssh-client.
  Installs ansible-core via pipx, runs pip install -r requirements.txt,
  runs ansible-galaxy collection install -r requirements.yml.
- **runtime stage**: only python3 + openssh-client apt packages.
  Copy /root/.local (pipx venv + wrappers) and /root/.ansible (collections)
  from builder. No compilers, no pip, no pipx binary, no git in final image.

## Acceptance criteria

- .devcontainer/Dockerfile uses FROM ... AS builder + second FROM for runtime
- Final image contains ansible-playbook, ansible-galaxy executables
- Final image does NOT contain pip, pipx, git binaries
- container-structure-test suite passes on the new image
- Image size reduced vs. current single-stage build (document before/after in commit)
close_reason: ubuntu:26.04 base adopted; all 6 container-structure-tests pass; image 686 MB

title: Fork-safe Docker CI: fix docker-publish.yml for fork compatibility
description:
## Why

docker-publish.yml hardcodes 'wonderbird/ansible-toolchain' as image name. On forks:
- Push steps fail (read-only GITHUB_TOKEN)
- Test jobs validate wrong image (upstream :latest, not the built one)
- Both architectures untested on PRs

Breaks CI for all contributors working on forks.

## What needs to be done

Implement the design from docs/features/fork-safe-docker-ci/:

1. Replace hardcoded image name with `${{ github.repository_owner }}/ansible-toolchain`
2. Split build into per-arch matrix: ubuntu-24.04 (amd64) + ubuntu-24.04-arm (arm64)
3. PR path: build → docker save tarball → upload artifact → test job downloads + loads
4. Non-PR path: push to ghcr.io → test job pulls from registry
5. Scope GHA cache keys per arch
6. Guard cosign/push steps with `github.event_name != 'pull_request'`

## Acceptance criteria

- PR from any fork: both archs build and test with no registry push
- Push to main: images published to fork owner's ghcr.io namespace
- No hardcoded 'wonderbird' in workflow file

title: Continue version-upgrade findings review — implementation phase
description: Documentation review completed. Resume findings collection starting with implementation findings. Previously collected: b3w (major version pinning), 546 (SDKMAN arch), 90n (constitution Principle II), 48h (tooling evaluation). Pending finding: version-update-playbooks.md repeats requirements instead of linking. Next phase: review implementation files for gaps.

title: Run all Molecule tests in build pipeline
description: No CI pipeline currently runs Molecule tests automatically. All roles with Molecule scenarios should be discovered and run on every PR or push to main. Need to design pipeline job (GitHub Actions or similar), handle podman availability in CI, and decide on parallelism strategy across roles.

title: Add pipeline job to check tracked tool version pins
description: query-versions.yml can detect stale version pins but is only run manually. Add a scheduled pipeline job (e.g. weekly GitHub Actions workflow) that runs query-versions.yml and reports or opens a PR when any pin is stale. Relates to the planned GitHub Actions integration noted in docs/architecture/version-update-playbooks.md Outlook.

title: Add no-version-history rule to constitution
description: Constitution should state that version history (changelogs, amendment logs) must not be added to rules files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, skill SKILL.md files). Exception: the constitution itself tracks versions per its governance section. Prevents rules files from accumulating stale history noise.

title: Constitution: add Fail-Loud principle (no silent skip / no empty fact fallthrough)
description:
Elevate the fail-loud NFR (currently spec.md FR-012 in feature 007-version-update-playbooks) into a cross-cutting constitution principle.

Decision (2026-05-12): Failure modes that silently skip work, return empty values, or proceed with unset/None facts are prohibited. Any task that fetches, parses, or computes a required value MUST fail with an explicit error identifying the source when the value cannot be obtained.

Scope to think through:
- Wording of the principle (e.g. Principle VIII: Fail Loud)
- MUST/SHOULD scope: applies to all roles, playbooks, task files
- Relationship to existing Principle I (Idempotency): fail-loud is orthogonal but reinforces predictability
- Version bump: MINOR (new principle)
- Sync impact: AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, templates, possibly fix-problem skill
- Once added: spec.md FR-012 in feature 007 can reference the principle instead of restating; per-task notes in tasks.md may simplify

Trigger: surfaced during /speckit.analyze on feature 007-version-update-playbooks where 3 of 4 fetch tasks lacked explicit parse-failure handling. User decision: not a feature-local concern — belongs in constitution.

title: Align documentation structure: technical vs product/feature split
description:
Define and apply consistent documentation taxonomy across the repo.

Decision (2026-05-12): technical concepts belong in docs/architecture/. Business logic and product aspects belong in feature/product documentation (structure TBD).

Scope to think through:
- What lives in docs/architecture/ (technical concepts, decisions, technical-debt, solution-strategy)
- What lives in docs/improvements/ today and whether it should move
- Where business-logic / product-aspect documentation goes (folder name + structure)
- Migration policy for existing docs that mix concerns
- Update review-documentation-here skill to encode the taxonomy

Trigger: surfaced during /speckit.analyze on feature 007-version-update-playbooks where spec.md and plan.md disagreed on concept-doc placement.

title: Make avoid-duplication principle explicit in constitution
description: The constitution references 'without duplicating logic' implicitly but does not state DRY/avoid-duplication as a named principle. Add it explicitly so agents and contributors have a clear mandate.

title: Verify MCP settings are backed up and restored correctly
description: wc0 fixed backup/restore exclusions but never confirmed what 'MCPs not backed up properly' meant. Need to: (1) identify where MCP server configs live on lorien (global settings.json vs per-project .mcp.json), (2) run a test backup+restore cycle, (3) confirm MCP configs survive round-trip.
close_reason: MCPs verified: no global MCPs configured; per-project .mcp.json is git-tracked; plugin MCP server reinstalled by omc update post-restore.

title: Fix Claude config backup/restore (MCPs, HUD)
description: Claude config backup incomplete — MCPs and HUD settings not backed up/restored properly. Reference guides: https://github.com/jtklinger/claude-code-backup-guide and https://dirkstrauss.com/claude-code-windows-migration-guide/ — crawl these to identify what files/dirs need backing up. Fix backup process to include all necessary config.
notes: Backup playbook updated: excluded credentials, ephemeral dirs (sessions, session-env, shell-snapshots, telemetry, downloads), plugins, hud, .omc/state, .omc/logs. Restore playbook deferred.
close_reason: Backup updated: excluded credentials, ephemeral dirs, plugins, hud, .omc state/logs. Restore updated: .claude.json now deleted before restore for clean state.

title: Windows: nvm not recognized after install — Node.js LTS install fails
description:
## Problem

Installing Node.js LTS via nvm fails on Windows. nvm is not recognized as a command despite being installed via Chocolatey.

## Error

Host: moria, task: Install Nodejs LTS

```
nvm : The term 'nvm' is not recognized as the name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or
operable program. Check the spelling of the name, or if a path was included, verify that the path
is correct and try again.
At line:1 char:65
+ ... le]::InputEncoding = New-Object Text.UTF8Encoding $false; nvm install ...
+                                                               ~~~
    + CategoryInfo          : ObjectNotFound: (nvm:String) [], CommandNotFoundException
    + FullyQualifiedErrorId : CommandNotFoundException
```

rc: 1

## Root Cause (hypothesis)

nvm installed via Chocolatey but PATH not refreshed in the current Ansible session. Windows requires a new shell session or explicit PATH reload after Chocolatey installs.

## Workaround

Disable the entire role `win_ai_agent` — not needed for current scope.

## Acceptance Criteria

- nvm is recognized in the same Ansible session after install
- `nvm install lts` completes successfully without manual intervention

title: Windows Bug Fixes and Improvements
description:
## Purpose

Umbrella epic for all Windows provisioning bugs and improvements discovered during hands-on sessions.

## Scope

Tracks issues found while manually provisioning and using the Windows (moria) host on AWS, including bugs, workarounds, and follow-up documentation tasks.

title: Windows provisioning fails: Chocolatey install denied (Win32ErrorCode 5)
description:
## Problem

Windows provisioning fails when installing Chocolatey due to missing permissions.

## Error

Host: moria

```
- name: Install Chocolatey
     ^ column 3

fatal: [moria]: FAILED! => {
    "changed": false,
    "cmd": "Set-ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Scope Process -Force\n[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [System.Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol -bor 3072\niex ((New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('https://community.chocolatey.org/install.ps1'))",
    "msg": "Failed to run: '': Exception calling \"CreateProcess\" with \"7\" argument(s): \"CreateProcessW() failed (Access is denied, Win32ErrorCode 5 - 0x00000005)\"",
    "rc": 5
}
```

## Root Cause (hypothesis)

Ansible is not running with Administrator privileges on the Windows target. `CreateProcessW` Win32ErrorCode 5 = Access Denied — process spawning blocked by insufficient permissions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Chocolatey installs successfully on moria without manual intervention
- Role validates or ensures elevated privileges before running install script
notes:
## Manual Workaround (resolved)

### Steps
1. Log in as Administrator on the Windows machine and reboot
2. After reboot, retry provisioning:
   ```bash
   ansible-playbook ./provision.yml --extra-vars "provider=aws platform=windows" -vvv >provision-aws-windows.log 2>&1
   ```

### Outcome
Problem resolved after step 1 (reboot as Administrator).

### AWS Note: Checking server readiness after reboot
Ping does NOT work on AWS Windows machines. Use nmap with -Pn to skip host discovery:
```bash
sudo nmap -v -sS -Pn -p 3389 $IPV4_ADDRESS
```
Port 3389 (RDP) open = machine ready. This applies to AWS machines in general.

title: Evaluate version-update mechanism: separation, Python rewrite, or external tool
description:
**Problem:** Current Ansible implementation has test coverage gaps, gets complex when adding tools, is fragile, hard to test, hard to extend. Open questions on structural direction before further investment.

**Why:** Implementation getting complex and fragile. Risk wasting effort on Ansible-based fixes (ypm, 2fr, g0k) if a different foundation is chosen.

**Questions to evaluate (merged from 48h):**
1. Should version check/upgrade tasks move to tool-specific subfolders for separation of concerns?
2. Should the mechanism be rewritten in Python with a plugin architecture to make adding new tools easier?
3. Is Renovate or another existing dependency-update tool a better fit than custom Ansible playbooks?

**Value:** Picking the right foundation enables easier testing, clearer logic, simpler extension, better error handling.

**Decision required:** Pick direction (keep Ansible refactor / Python rewrite / external tool) before making Ansible-based changes that might become obsolete.

**Note:** This must be resolved before ypm, 2fr, g0k to avoid wasted effort. Merged from 48h on 2026-05-13.

title: Eliminate duplication between query-versions.yml and perform-updates.yml
description:
**Problem:** Duplication between playbooks/update-versions/query-versions.yml and perform-updates.yml causes changes needed in multiple files, risks inconsistency, and obscures source of truth.

**Why:** Future maintainability, contradicts 'no duplication' principle.

**Value:** Single source of truth, easier updates, clearer intent, easier to understand and less confusion.

**Solution:** Extract common patterns from both playbooks into shared components or interfaces.

title: Refactor playbooks/tasks/query-versions.yml for tool-based grouping with interface pattern
description:
**Problem:** Current phase-based grouping causes scattered logic and requires changes across multiple sections when adding new tools.

**Why:** Improving future maintainability.

**Value:** Tool-based grouping with interface definition enables single point of extension, applying Open/Closed Principle, high cohesion and loose coupling — concepts easier to understand and implement.

**Solution:** Regroup query-versions.yml by tool instead of phase. Each tool implements a common interface (read pins, extract pins, fetch upstream, compare and report, fail if stale). Encapsulate tool-specific steps as separate tasklists.

title: Extend new-role.sh for version-update pattern consideration
description:
**Problem:** New roles risk installing tools at outdated versions, introducing security risks.

**Why:** Improving consistency for future role creation.

**Value:** Avoid forgetting to consider version updates when creating new roles.

**Solution:** Extend new-role.sh script and templates to incorporate playbooks/update-versions concept so new roles bake in version-update tasks from creation.

title: Finding: constitution/role checklist must mandate registering new tools in version-update playbooks
description: When a new role is created that installs a versioned tool, there is currently no rule requiring the author to add that tool to the version-update mechanism (fetch task file, query-versions.yml, perform-updates.yml, concept doc). This gap means new tools silently escape version tracking. The constitution or a role-creation checklist must mandate: for every new role that pins a version in defaults/main.yml, a corresponding fetch task and playbook wiring must be added.

title: Finding: document maintenance-playbook exception to Principle II in constitution
description: The justified exception allowing maintenance playbooks (procedural localhost operator tools) to contain inline logic rather than wrapping roles is only recorded in per-feature plan.md Complexity Tracking tables. It should be canonically stated in the constitution under Principle II so future agents apply it consistently. Name the pattern explicitly and reference playbooks/backup/, playbooks/restore/, playbooks/update-versions/ as precedents. Discuss exact wording when fixing.

title: Finding: SDKMAN query hardcoded to linuxx64, may miss arm64 availability
description: fetch-java-version.yml queries https://api.sdkman.io/2/candidates/java/linuxx64/versions/all. Temurin version strings are typically arch-agnostic in SDKMAN, but availability can diverge between amd64 and arm64. First step: verify whether a linuxarm64 endpoint exists in the SDKMAN API. If yes: query both and take intersection to find highest version available on all target architectures (amd64 hobbiton/rivendell, arm64 lorien). If no: keep current impl but document the limitation in docs/architecture/version-update-playbooks.md.

title: Finding: all tools need explicit major version pin
description: Only Java enforces same-major-patch tracking via java_sdkman_major. Flutter, gitmux, Nerd Fonts, and Android have no major version pin in role defaults. A major upstream bump passes silently through both playbooks. Each tool needs a pinned major variable in defaults and query-versions.yml should detect major-boundary crossings with a distinct error, separate from normal staleness.

title: Configure major version pinning for all tracked tools
description: Currently only Java pins major version explicitly (java_sdkman_major derived from java_sdkman_identifier). Other tools (Flutter, gitmux, Nerd Fonts, Android) have no major version pin, so a major version bump upstream would silently pass through query-versions.yml and perform-updates.yml without any warning. Need a mechanism to pin the expected major for each tool and detect when upstream crosses a major boundary, keeping major upgrades a deliberate manual decision.
close_reason: Duplicate of b3w (finding tracks same work — major version pinning for all tools). Per user 2026-05-13.

Review the full workflow: how agents discover and read the molecule test rule, whether the skill frontmatter triggers correctly in Claude Code context, and whether the rule index in AGENTS.md points to the skill correctly. Subtask of ansible-all-my-things-l10.

Check that deleted cursor rules are not referenced anywhere, new skill files are properly indexed, and AGENTS.md reflects the actual state of the workspace.

title: Docs: AWS machines don't respond to ping — use nmap -Pn to check readiness
description:
## Why

AWS EC2 instances (including Windows) do not respond to ICMP ping. This trips up anyone waiting for a machine to be ready after boot or reboot.

Discovered while troubleshooting Windows provisioning (see ansible-all-my-things-dev Chocolatey bug).

## What to document

Add a note to the AWS docs explaining:
- Ping does NOT work on AWS machines
- Use nmap with -Pn (skip host discovery) to check reachability:
  ```shell
  # Windows (RDP port)
  sudo nmap -v -sS -Pn -p 3389 $IPV4_ADDRESS

  # Linux (SSH port)
  sudo nmap -v -sS -Pn -p 22 $IPV4_ADDRESS
  ```
- Port shown as 'open' = instance ready

## Where to add

- test/test_aws_provisioning.md — Step 1, after the provisioning commands (waiting for readiness context)
- docs/user-manual/prerequisites-aws.md — as operational tip

title: Evaluate separation and tooling for version-check/upgrade mechanism
description: Reminder to revisit the version-update-playbooks design. Questions to evaluate: (1) Should version check/upgrade tasks move to tool-specific subfolders for separation of concerns? (2) Should the mechanism be rewritten in Python with a plugin architecture to make adding new tools easier? (3) Is Renovate or another existing dependency-update tool a better fit than custom Ansible playbooks?
close_reason: Merged into 3gu — 3gu expanded to cover all three evaluation questions (separation, Python rewrite, Renovate). Per user 2026-05-13.

title: Clarify upgrade behavior when version pins change and configure-linux re-runs
description: After perform-updates.yml bumps version pins in role defaults, re-running configure-linux.yml raises an open question: do roles replace the existing installed version, install side-by-side, or leave the old version in place? Need to audit each tracked tool's install task to determine current behavior, decide the desired policy, and document or fix per tool. Tools: Flutter, gitmux, Nerd Fonts (Hack), Android cmdline-tools, Java (Temurin via SDKMAN). Decision may be role-specific rather than a single global policy.

title: Establish update mechanism for pinned version numbers in role defaults
description: Role defaults/*.yml files contain pinned version numbers (e.g. package versions, binary versions). No mechanism exists to detect when these go stale or to update them systematically. Need to establish a way to check upstream and update pinned versions across all roles.

title: Replace command blocks in productContext.md §How It Works with user-manual link
description: Three fixes in one pass: (1) docs/productContext.md §How It Works + §Primary Workflows: replace bash command blocks and step-by-step workflow lists with a concise summary sentence + cross-reference to docs/user-manual/create-vm.md. productContext.md is a product context doc (why/what/who), not operational how-to. (2) docs/productContext.md §Success Indicators §Quantitative Measures: the 'Cost Optimization Across Providers' line currently points to README.md — wrong. Change to point to vars.yml files in inventories, e.g. inventories/group_vars/hcloud_linux/vars.yml (has pricing table comment + hetzner.com link). (3) docs/features/consistent-provisioning-style/concept.md §Provider Differences table: remove entirely. Table has cost figures (ruled out), duplicates README.md VM table, and is out of scope for a provisioning-interface concept doc. The planned-work pointer line below it can stay.

title: Consolidate VM inventory tables into README.md, remove costs from docs
description: README.md and docs/techContext.md contain redundant VM tables. Consolidate into README.md only, remove from techContext.md. Cost figures must not appear in docs — refer to vars.yml comments instead. Steps: (1) Expand README.md VM table to include region + auth chain columns; (2) Add one-line cost pointer: 'For current pricing see vars.yml comments, e.g. inventories/group_vars/hcloud_linux/vars.yml (Hetzner) and AWS pricing pages linked from inventories/group_vars/aws_ec2_linux/vars.yml'; (3) Remove from techContext.md: §Per-VM Specifications table, §Cost Analysis (both occurrences in §Success Indicators and §Infrastructure Requirements), §Provider Differences Reference table; (4) Replace removed techContext.md sections with one-line pointer to README.md table; (5) Verify no cost figures remain in docs/ or README.md via grep.

title: Fix vault file reference in techContext.md Idiomatic Configuration section
description: docs/techContext.md has vars.yml/vault.yml confusion in TWO places: (1) §Idiomatic Configuration (line ~381 before section deletion): incorrectly states secrets stored in group_vars/all/vars.yml as vault-encrypted; (2) §Inventory Directory Tree (line 94): labels vars.yml as '# Vault-encrypted shared secrets'. In both places: vars.yml is a plain wrapper; group_vars/all/vault.yml (user-created, not in repo) is the encrypted file. Fix both: correct file references to vault.yml, clarify vars.yml is a plain wrapper referencing vault.yml, mention vault-template.yml as the template users copy, add cross-reference to docs/user-manual/important-concepts.md for setup instructions.

title: Delete superseded provision-aws-*.yml wrappers and update doc references
description: provision-aws-linux.yml and provision-aws-windows.yml are thin wrappers superseded by the unified provisioning command (provision.yml --extra-vars provider=aws platform=linux/windows). grep confirms only two docs reference them: docs/productContext.md (lines 84, 91) and docs/techContext.md (lines 51-52). Steps: (1) delete provision-aws-linux.yml and provision-aws-windows.yml; (2) update docs/productContext.md How It Works section to show unified command; (3) update docs/techContext.md Project Structure tree to remove the two entries. Verify no remaining references via grep after changes.

title: Scope 'complete environment cleanup' claim to cloud providers in productContext
description: docs/productContext.md §Success Indicators / Qualitative Measures: the 'Complete environment cleanup' bullet claims universal coverage but local Vagrant environments (dagorlad Docker, lorien Tart) incur no cloud cost and are excluded from teardown pressure. Rephrase to explicitly scope the cleanup guarantee to cloud providers (Hetzner, AWS) and note Vagrant environments are excluded.

title: Remove unimplemented vagrant_docker sections from consistent-provisioning concept
description: docs/features/consistent-provisioning-style/concept.md currently describes vagrant_docker provisioner (Command Reference entry, Vagrant Docker Specifics section, Provider Differences row) as if implemented. provisioners/vagrant_docker-linux.yml does not exist. Per review-documentation: stable concept docs describe system as-is only. Remove all vagrant_docker-specific sections. Add one-line reference pointing to docs/feature-requests/feat.consistent.provisioning.style/prd.md for the unimplemented extension. Same check for Tart.

title: Remove stale kiro branch section from feat.kiro prd
description: docs/feature-requests/feat.kiro/prd.md §2 describes porting enhancements from a kiro branch that no longer exists. Remove §2 entirely. Also remove corresponding section from solution-design.md. §1 (Install Kiro IDE Role) stays.

title: Clean up docs/feature-requests documentation
description:
Memory bank branches have been moved to docs/feature-requests. Each subfolder follows the memory-bank-by-cline skill concept. Many redundant files exist across subfolders and need consolidation.

Work:
1. Audit all subfolders in docs/feature-requests (each is a memory bank)
2. Remove obsolete information
3. Merge important information into relevant target files
4. Final structure must follow the review-documentation-here and review-documentation skill concepts
5. When done, run the review-documentation-here skill to validate final documentation structure
close_reason: Refactored docs/feature-requests/ into prd+solution-design. Bundled Kiro folders. Renamed typo folder. Rewrote top-level productContext.md+techContext.md. Verified via review-documentation-here skill (PASS, 1 broken link fixed). Plan + audit at .omc/plans/4fv-feature-requests-refactor.md.

title: Map project-specific skills to ai-agent-workspace roles
description: Review which project-specific skills in this repo can be linked to modified roles or skills in the ai-agent-workspace repository. Examples: memory-bank-branches (510-memory-bank-branches.mdc deleted here), project-specific-documentation-strategy (610 rule). Identify gaps and decide whether to update, remove, or create new skill files.

title: Fix deprecated Ansible usage pattern warnings in playbooks
description: When running playbooks, Ansible emits deprecation warnings about usage patterns that will break in future versions. These warnings should be identified and fixed across all playbooks to ensure forward compatibility.

value: fetch-android-version.yml uses proximity regex ([\s\S]{0,400}?) to find SHA-1 near build number in HTML — fragile, tracked as TD-009, isolated per FR-007

scripts/test-molecule-all.sh runs molecule test for every role with a molecule/default/molecule.yml scenario, collects pass/fail results, and exits 1 if any failed. Use this in CI instead of reimplementing role discovery.
